title: 10 Artemis II photos that define humanity's return to the moon
summary: From spectacular views of Earth to a unique total solar eclipse, Artemis II's most breathtaking images tell the story of humanity's return to deep space after more than 50 years.

title: مادة أطول من قطار
summary: تستحق المادة 1ـ40 من قانون المسطرة الجنائية المحين حديثا، دخول كتاب غينيس مرفوعة الهامة، باعتبارها أطول مادة في كل القوانين، بل لعلها أطول من قطار. تحتل المادة المذكورة صفحتين اثنتين كاملتين من أكثر من 76 فقرة تبتدئ كلها بلازمات (يجوز ويحق ويترتب ويمكن ويأمر ويتعين وغيرها). معلومأكمل القراءة » المق…
body: تستحق المادة 1ـ40 من قانون المسطرة الجنائية المحين حديثا، دخول كتاب غينيس مرفوعة الهامة، باعتبارها أطول مادة في كل القوانين، بل لعلها أطول من قطار. تحتل المادة المذكورة صفحتين اثنتين كاملتين من أكثر من 76 فقرة تبتدئ كلها بلازمات (يجوز ويحق ويترتب ويمكن ويأمر ويتعين وغيرها). معلوم أكمل القراءة » المقالة مادة أطول من قطار fمنشورة على جريدة الصباح .

title: 'I'm at a loss for words': Artemis II mission comes home to joy and cheers after historic 10-day mission
summary: NASA's 10-day moon mission has officially ended with a "bullseye landing"

title: فيديو.. هبوط رواد "أرتيميس 2" في مياه المحيط الهادئ
summary: هبطت مركبة مهمة "أرتيميس 2" التي حملت 4 رواد فضاء حول القمر، الجمعة، في مياه المحيط الهادئ مقابل سواحل كاليفورنيا، كما هو مخطط لها.

title: There are 'reasons to be confident' about faulty Artemis II heat shield ahead of 25,000 mph reentry, space expert Ed Macaulay says
summary: NASA's Artemis II heat shield is about to face its ultimate test as the Orion crew prepares for reentry. Physics and data science lecturer Ed Macaulay tells Live Science reasons to be confident ahead of today's historic splashdown.

title: The moon is green and brown? Why scientists are already excited about Artemis II's historic lunar photos
summary: As Earth reels at the beauty of Artemis II's historic lunar flyby photos, geologists working on the mission are excited for big insights about the moon.

title: 'More questions than answers': Experts baffled by Alaskan mammal-eating orcas spotted near Seattle
summary: Three orcas from Alaska surfaced in the waters between Washington state and Canada in March, an area where they've never been documented.

title: Aoshima: Japan's tiny 'Cat Island' where felines hugely outnumber humans
summary: Once a thriving sardine fishing island, today Aoshima is home to roughly 80 cats and just a handful of people who look after the felines with the help of food donations from around Japan.

title: How and When to Watch the Artemis II Mission’s Return to Earth
summary: Splashdown for the Artemis II crew is scheduled for Friday evening, and NASA will livestream the historic homecoming.

title: Ancient Korean society practiced human sacrifice and high inbreeding, researchers find
summary: A genomic analysis of dozens of ancient Korean skeletons revealed a special "sacrificial caste" of people.

title: China Is Cracking Down on Scams. Just Not the Ones Hitting Americans
summary: As Beijing clamps down on fraud at home, researchers say crime syndicates are shifting their focus to victims elsewhere.

title: على شكل "كرة نار".. رواد الفضاء يستعدون للعودة إلى الأرض
summary: يستعد رواد الفضاء الذين أرسلتهم وكالة الفضاء الأميركية "ناسا" إلى القمر في إطار مهمة "أرتيمس 2" للعودة إلى الأرض، وذلك بعد انطلاقهم في مطلع أبريل الجاري.

title: James Webb telescope spots 'stingray' galaxy system that could solve the mystery of 'little red dots'
summary: A study of the fascinating galaxy system nicknamed "The Stingray" suggests that mysterious little red dots could be a phase in the evolution of galaxies powered by actively feeding black holes, rather than a distinct class of objects.

title: 'RIP, Comet MAPS': Watch the superbright sungrazer become a 'headless wonder' after being ripped apart by the sun
summary: New images show the sungrazer comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS) did not survive its close approach to our home star. Instead, the celestial object briefly turned into a "headless wonder" before fully disintegrating.

title: How to see Comet PanSTARRS as it brightens in the night sky this week
summary: Comet C/2025 R3 (PanSTARRS) could be visible to the naked eye in the predawn sky after April 13 as it makes a close approach to the sun.

title: 'They are literally everywhere': The shocking story of how forever chemicals polluted the world
summary: Live Science spoke with Mariah Blake, an investigative journalist and author of the book "They Poisoned The World," about one of the greatest corporate scandals in history.

title: 'So much magic': Artemis II shares first images from the far side of the moon, including new 'Earthset' and total eclipse in space
summary: NASA's first set of images captured by the Artemis II crew during their lunar flyby are here, and they're stunning.

title: World's fattest parrot — on the verge of extinction 30 years ago — has record-breaking breeding season
summary: Conservationists are celebrating the 105th kākāpō chick to hatch during the 2026 breeding season — the highest number reported since such records began 30 years ago.

title: Deadly, vivid-green mass sprawls across South African reservoir — Earth from space
summary: A 2022 satellite photo shows a thick mat of blooming algae and invasive aquatic plants spreading across the surface of the reservoir at South Africa's Hartebeespoort Dam. The verdant mass is both toxic and capable of depleting the water's oxygen levels.

title: The Artemis II astronauts have just flown farther from Earth than any humans in history
summary: Artemis II is now the farthest crewed mission from Earth in history. The occasion was marked by a number of poignant moments.

title: Artemis II moon flyby begins: How to watch and what to know
summary: The Artemis II astronauts have reached the moon and will soon lose contact with NASA as they whip around the lunar far side. Here's how to follow along with their journey and everything you need to know about the "dark side" of the moon.

title: 'They could spend 4 or 5 hours per day underwater': How humans adapted to the most challenging environments
summary: In the book "Adaptable," evolutionary anthropologist Herman Pontzer explores human biology and development, and how people have evolved to survive everywhere on Earth.

title: The hungriest black holes in the universe are running out of food, survey of 8,000 cosmic monsters reveals
summary: Astronomers studied 1.3 million galaxies and 8,000 X-ray-spewing supermassive black holes to find out why these gravitational monsters are growing more slowly than ever.

title: Beadnet dress: A 4,500-year-old ancient Egyptian funeral 'gown' that was in vogue during the Old Kingdom
summary: This rare example of an ancient Egyptian beaded dress was likely crafted specifically for a woman's funeral.

title: 'This generation's moment': How the Artemis missions will reframe humanity's relationship with the moon
summary: Live Science spoke with Rebecca Boyle, author of "Our Moon" about how the moon has been viewed both culturally and scientifically through history.

title: NASA telescope uncovers new mystery in supernova first spotted by Chinese astronomers 2,000 years ago —‬ Space photo of the week
summary: NASA's Imaging X-ray Polarimetry Explorer reveals the expansion and shock patterns within RCW 86, a supernova observed by early astronomers in A.D. 185.

title: What happened to the Minoan civilization?
summary: The Minoan civilization flourished between roughly 2000 and 1500 B.C. on Crete and nearby islands. How did it come to an end?

title: 10 ملايين دولار.. مكافأة أميركية لاعتقال شقيقين يقودان عصابة
summary: قالت وزارة الخارجية الأميركية يوم الخميس إنها ستدفع ما يصل إلى 10 ملايين دولار مقابل معلومات تؤدي إلى اعتقال أو إدانة شقيقين تم تحديدهما كزعيمين لعصابة سينالوا المكسيكية في ولاية باها كاليفورنيا، التي تضم مدينة تيخوانا.
